title: Shared dialog source description
description: Replayable description of a shared AI dialog captured from a provider such as ChatGPT or Google AI Mode. The schema represents both successful captures (with turns) and unsupported-capture diagnostics, and is designed so formal-ai can map it to demo_memory events and web-capture can emit it directly. See README.md for the equivalent meta-language (LiNo) form.

visibility.description: Whether the turn was shown to the end user or was internal (e.g. reasoning, tool traffic).

sharedDialogTurn.description: A single message in the dialog.

turn_id.description: Stable identifier of the turn within this capture.

order.description: Zero-based position of the turn in the transcript.

source_fragment.description: Raw source slice or selector the turn was extracted from, for replay/debugging.

sharedDialogCaptureDiagnostic.description: Explanation for an unsuccessful or partial capture.

diagnostic_code.description: Machine-readable reason. Mirrors a non-captured capture_status value or a finer-grained provider-specific code.

evidence.description: Optional raw evidence (HTML snippet, screenshot reference, log excerpt) supporting the diagnostic.

source_url.description: Canonical URL of the shared dialog. Null when the source has no URL (e.g. a pasted Markdown transcript).

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the capture was performed.

turns.description: Captured turns in transcript order. Present (and non-empty) when capture_status is "captured".

diagnostics.description: Diagnostics explaining a non-captured or partial result.
